message:
bd-2ke: QuickJS promise bridge + job draining semantics

Implement the Promise-based hostcall bridge for PiJS runtime:

- Add PiJsRuntime combining QuickJS + Scheduler + Promise bridge
- Implement pi.* methods (tool, exec, http, session, ui, events) via
  native Rust functions returning call_id strings
- Create JS bridge that wraps native functions in Promise-returning wrappers
- Add HostcallRequest queue for host-side processing
- Wire scheduler macrotask delivery to resolve/reject Promises
- Add automatic microtask draining after each macrotask
- Add json_to_js and js_to_json conversion helpers
- Add HostcallKind enum for typed hostcall dispatch
- Add PiJsTickStats for tick execution statistics
- Add 5 integration tests for Promise resolution/rejection

Architecture uses two-layer design to avoid lifetime issues:
1. Rust native functions (__pi_*_native) return call_id strings
2. JS wrappers create Promises and register them with call_id

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2ikc","title":"Editor: @ fuzzy file reference insertion","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `@` file reference UX: typing `@` triggers fuzzy-search project files.

# Behavioral Spec
- As the user types after `@`, suggestions narrow using fuzzy matching.
- Selecting a file inserts a usable reference into the editor.

# Open Design Choice (resolve in implementation)
Legacy mentions “File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files”.
We must choose one of:
1) Insert `@relative/path` (explicit marker), OR
2) Insert `relative/path` but still treat it as an attachment later.

Pick one and document it; ensure it works with existing file-context inclusion code.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can attach files without leaving the keyboard.
- [ ] Inserted reference is correct relative to cwd and works across platforms.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:34.583235605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:40.656752819Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:34.589692407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-28rk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:40.656707685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-28t8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:40.061362319Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jdz","title":"Interactive: implement /new (start new session without restart)","description":"# Goal
Implement `/new` inside interactive mode to start a fresh session without restarting the process.

# Required Behavior
- Clear the current conversation view.
- Create a new Session:
  - new session id
  - new JSONL file path (if persistence enabled)
  - header uses current cwd, model/thinking settings
- Reset agent message history to an empty session context.

# UX
- Preserve loaded resources (skills/prompts/themes) from current run.
- Provide a status message indicating new session started.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/new` results in a clean slate session.
- [ ] Persistence works (new file created, not overwriting old).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:34.248604864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:05:20.484206305Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:05:20.484141665Z","close_reason":"Implemented /new interactive command; resets conversation + new session persistence","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jdz","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:34.255254144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jh","title":"Implement error handling path tests","description":"# Implement error handling path tests

## Goal
Test all error conditions that can occur during normal operation, with detailed logging.

## Background
Error paths are currently untested but critical for UX:
- Network failures
- API rate limits
- Authentication failures
- Malformed responses
- File system errors
- Timeout scenarios

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- All API error cases must use VCR playback (no live network).
- File system errors must use temp dirs and deterministic paths.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log cassette path, error category, and sanitized error details.
- On failure, dump expected vs actual error variants + any response body fragments.

## Test Categories

### Network Errors
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_connection_refused() {
    let vcr = VcrRecorder::new("network_connection_refused");
    let provider = AnthropicProvider::new_with_client(vcr.client());

    let result = provider.stream(&context, &options).await;
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(Error::Network { .. })));
}
```

### API Errors
- 429 rate limit (retry-after)
- 401 auth failure
- 403 forbidden
- 400 bad request
- 500 server error
- 529 overloaded

### Malformed Response Errors
- Invalid JSON body
- Missing required fields
- Unexpected SSE event type
- Truncated stream

### File System Errors
- Read permission denied
- Write permission denied
- Disk full (simulate with tempfs limits if feasible)
- Path too long

### Tool Execution Errors
- bash command not found
- bash timeout
- edit file not found

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure for API error testing)

## Files
- tests/error_handling.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 25+ error handling tests
- [ ] All network errors covered (via VCR/local simulation)
- [ ] All API error codes covered
- [ ] File system errors covered
- [ ] Tool errors covered
- [ ] Logs include error category + cassette id
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:33.110276433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:34.660912407Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:41:01.345001879Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:33.123723281Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:34.660884836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:32.812857475Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2ke","title":"Impl: QuickJS promise bridge + job draining semantics","description":"# Goal
Wire QuickJS async runtime to the PiJS event loop with a **correct Promise/hostcall bridge** and explicit job draining.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `host.call` returns a JS Promise backed by a Rust completion handle.
- Job draining loop: run pending jobs, then poll timers/hostcalls per spec.
- Cancellation propagation: cancel token to hostcall, reject Promise with mapped error.
- Explicit error mapping + stack traces for debugging.
- Structured logs per hostcall with correlation IDs.

# Correctness Notes
- No re-entrancy hazards: only drain jobs at safe points.
- Promise resolution order stable with scheduler sequence ids.

# Tests
- Unit tests for Promise resolution ordering.
- Conformance fixtures for cancellation/timeouts.
- E2E lifecycle harness asserts log ordering.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:13.010362542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:38:27.700848180Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:47.700708791Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:48.163859792Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-8mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:48.673842173Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Added 5 integration tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:47.700708791Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:48.163859792Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-8mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:48.673842173Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ki","title":"Tooling: Compatibility rewrite map + extc contract","description":"# Goal
Define the **compatibility contract** that maps legacy Node/Bun imports to PiJS shims so **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits).

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions** in extc rewrites.
- Rewrites must be defined solely in terms of import specifiers and generic, semantics-preserving code patterns.
- If the sample reveals a gap, fix it by adding a **general rule + tests**, not by branching on extension id.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Canonical rewrite map for imports + globals.
- Explicitly forbidden APIs with clear diagnostics.
- Extc input/output contract: ESM output, shim injection rules, side-effect policy.
- Compatibility matrix of supported APIs and required capabilities.
- Source map contract for accurate error locations.

# Canonical rewrite / resolution rules
## Import specifiers
Extc must ensure every import specifier is resolvable inside PiJS without Node/Bun.

Recommended canonicalization:

### A) Node builtins (`node:*`)
Rewrite `node:*` builtins to an internal namespace that PiJS provides (so bundlers don’t externalize them):
- `node:fs`              -> `pi:node/fs`
- `node:fs/promises`     -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `node:path`            -> `pi:node/path`
- `node:os`              -> `pi:node/os`
- `node:url`             -> `pi:node/url`
- `node:crypto`          -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `node:child_process`   -> `pi:node/child_process` (or rewrite usage to `pi.exec` when semantics preserved)
- `node:module`          -> `pi:node/module`

### B) Bare builtins (`'fs'`, `'path'`, ...)
Many real-world deps import builtins without the `node:` prefix. Treat these identically:
- `fs`            -> `pi:node/fs`
- `fs/promises`   -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `path`          -> `pi:node/path`
- `os`            -> `pi:node/os`
- `url`           -> `pi:node/url`
- `crypto`        -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `child_process` -> `pi:node/child_process`
- `module`        -> `pi:node/module`

## Global polyfills (injection)
Extc may inject an idempotent prelude import at the bundle entrypoint:
- `import 'pi:polyfills/node_globals'` (installs `process`, `Buffer`, etc.)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/fetch'` (if needed)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/webassembly'` (assume QuickJS has no wasm; installs PiWasm bridge)

Injection must be:
- deterministic (stable ordering)
- sourcemap-correct
- versioned (shim_version included in artifact hash)

# Forbidden / flagged APIs
- The contract must list:
  - **Forbidden** (hard error): APIs that bypass capability policy or escape sandbox.
  - **Flagged** (warning w/ evidence): risky constructs (dynamic eval, unbounded recursion, etc.).

Notes:
- The pinned sample includes `new Function(...)` for loading a bundled script; do not forbid it. Prefer “flag + log” unless we can prove safe.

# Compatibility matrix (must be sample-driven)
- The matrix must enumerate the exact Node builtins/globals required by the pinned sample set and define:
  - supported functions
  - sync vs async semantics
  - error mapping expectations
  - capability requirements + scopes

Implementation beads:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0
- node:child_process subset: bd-2sr
- WebAssembly bridge (PiWasm): bd-1ry

# Tests
- Unit transform fixtures for common imports and injection.
- Negative tests for forbidden APIs with exact messages.
- E2E harness verifies rewritten bundles run 16/16 with actionable failures.

# Dependencies
- Must align with the PiJS runtime contract + event loop semantics (bd-123) and hostcall ABI (bd-37z).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:59.736950487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:21:50.124812910Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:21:50.124743731Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added §2A Extc Compatibility Contract to EXTENSIONS.md with canonical rewrite rules, global polyfill injection, forbidden/flagged APIs, compatibility matrix, and sourcemap contract","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:05.067354913Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:05.533406188Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2knt","title":"Interactive: implement in-app /resume session picker","description":"# Goal
Implement `/resume` inside interactive mode to open the session picker UI and resume a selected session **without restarting**.

# Required Behavior
- `/resume` opens a picker overlay/component.
- Selecting a session:
  - loads the JSONL session file
  - updates agent message history to the selected leaf
  - rebuilds the conversation viewport
  - updates footer/session metadata

# Implementation Notes
- Reuse `src/session_picker.rs` where possible.
- When non-TTY, CLI flag `-r/--resume` already handles picker; this task is specifically the in-app slash command.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/resume` works from a running interactive session.
- [ ] After selection, the app behaves as if started with `--session <path>`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:13.400481100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:19:31.224893087Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2knt","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:13.409450504Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2knt","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:42:34.029299582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2lg","title":"Implement compaction module unit tests","description":"# Implement compaction module unit tests

## Goal
Add comprehensive unit tests for src/compaction.rs (903 lines, currently 0 tests).

## Background
Compaction is a critical feature that:
- Summarizes old conversation history to save context tokens
- Identifies optimal cut points in message history
- Tracks file operations (read/write/edit) for summary
- Uses LLM to generate summaries

## Module Analysis (src/compaction.rs)

### Key Types
```rust
struct CompactionContext {
    entries: Vec<SessionEntry>,
    config: CompactionConfig,
    token_estimator: TokenEstimator,
}

struct CutPoint {
    entry_index: usize,
    reason: CutPointReason,
    tokens_before: usize,
    tokens_after: usize,
}

enum CutPointReason {
    TokenLimit,
    UserMessageBoundary,
    ToolResultBoundary,
    NaturalBreak,
}

struct FileSummary {
    path: String,
    operations: Vec<FileOp>,
}
```

### Key Functions
- `estimate_tokens(entry: &SessionEntry) -> usize`
- `find_cut_points(entries: &[SessionEntry], config: &Config) -> Vec<CutPoint>`
- `extract_file_operations(entries: &[SessionEntry]) -> Vec<FileSummary>`
- `prepare_compaction(entries: &[SessionEntry], config: &Config) -> CompactionPlan`
- `generate_summary_prompt(entries: &[SessionEntry], files: &[FileSummary]) -> String`

## Test Categories

### 1. Token Estimation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_simple_text() {
    let entry = user_message("Hello, world!");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    assert!(tokens > 0 && tokens < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_code_block() {
    let entry = assistant_message("```rust\nfn main() {}\n```");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    // Code blocks should estimate higher
}

#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_tool_result() {
    let entry = tool_result("read", "Large file content...");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    // Verify reasonable estimate
}
```

### 2. Cut Point Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_find_cut_points_token_limit() {
    let entries = generate_entries(100, 1000);  // 100 entries, ~1000 tokens each
    let config = CompactionConfig { max_tokens: 50000 };
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should find cuts when exceeding limit
    assert!(!cuts.is_empty());
    assert!(cuts[0].tokens_after <= config.max_tokens);
}

#[test]
fn test_cut_point_prefers_user_message_boundary() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Q1"),
        assistant_message("A1"),
        user_message("Q2"),  // Preferred cut point
        assistant_message("A2"),
    ];
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &low_limit_config());
    
    // Should cut at user message boundary
    assert_eq!(cuts[0].reason, CutPointReason::UserMessageBoundary);
}

#[test]
fn test_cut_point_preserves_tool_pairs() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Read file"),
        assistant_with_tool_call("read", { "path": "test.txt" }),
        tool_result("read", "File contents..."),
        assistant_message("The file contains..."),
    ];
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should not cut between tool call and result
    for cut in cuts {
        assert_ne!(cut.entry_index, 2);  // Not after tool call
    }
}
```

### 3. File Operation Extraction Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_read() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "src/main.rs", "lines_read": 100})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert_eq!(files.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(files[0].path, "src/main.rs");
    assert_eq!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Read { lines: 100 });
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_write() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("write", json!({"path": "new.txt", "bytes_written": 1234})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert_eq!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Write { bytes: 1234 });
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_edit() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("edit", json!({
            "path": "src/lib.rs",
            "old_length": 10,
            "new_length": 15
        })),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert!(matches!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Edit { .. }));
}

#[test]
fn test_file_operations_grouped_by_path() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "a.txt"})),
        tool_result_with_details("edit", json!({"path": "a.txt"})),
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "b.txt"})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    // Files should be deduplicated
    assert_eq!(files.len(), 2);
    assert_eq!(files.iter().find(|f| f.path == "a.txt").unwrap().operations.len(), 2);
}
```

### 4. Summary Prompt Generation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_summary_prompt_includes_messages() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Help me with X"),
        assistant_message("I'll help with X by doing Y"),
    ];
    
    let prompt = generate_summary_prompt(&entries, &[]);
    
    assert!(prompt.contains("Help me with X"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("Y"));
}

#[test]
fn test_summary_prompt_includes_file_summary() {
    let files = vec![
        FileSummary {
            path: "src/main.rs".into(),
            operations: vec![FileOp::Read { lines: 100 }, FileOp::Edit { old: 10, new: 15 }],
        },
    ];
    
    let prompt = generate_summary_prompt(&[], &files);
    
    assert!(prompt.contains("src/main.rs"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("read"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("edit"));
}
```

### 5. Compaction Plan Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_prepare_compaction_identifies_entries_to_keep() {
    let entries = generate_entries(50, 1000);
    let config = CompactionConfig {
        max_tokens: 30000,
        keep_recent: 10000,
    };
    
    let plan = prepare_compaction(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should keep recent entries
    assert!(plan.entries_to_keep.len() < entries.len());
    assert!(plan.tokens_after_compaction <= config.max_tokens);
}
```

## Dependencies
None (unit tests only)

## Files
- src/compaction.rs (add #[cfg(test)] module)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 20+ unit tests for compaction
- [ ] Token estimation tested
- [ ] Cut point detection tested
- [ ] File operation extraction tested
- [ ] Summary prompt generation tested
- [ ] Edge cases covered
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:36:53.592325399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:23:36.355281684Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:23:36.355215541Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lg","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:36:53.598326111Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-320","title":"Implement JS compatibility pipeline (bundle -> runtime)","description":"Background:
- Many extensions are JS/TS; we need a compatibility tier to run them as-is.
- Assume **QuickJS has no WebAssembly**: JS compatibility must be hybrid (QuickJS for JS + wasmtime-backed PiWasm for wasm).

Steps:
- Lock the JS execution strategy for the pinned sample:
  - QuickJS bytecode for JS execution.
  - PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry) for `globalThis.WebAssembly` expectations.
  - JS→WASM is optional/future; do not block sample success on it.
- Implement build pipeline integration + caching by hash (extc/SWC) and artifact metadata.
- Provide/ensure shims for legacy APIs (Node core + globals) map into connector hostcalls.
- Integrate with the QuickJS runtime and connector dispatcher.
- Ensure module loading contract supports shim modules and deterministic resolution.

Acceptance (hard):
- **All 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits), end-to-end under the conformance/E2E harness.

Notes:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0 + bd-2sr.
- WebAssembly bridge: bd-1ry.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:23:48.829161504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:39:10.831664521Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-320","depends_on_id":"bd-1f5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:51.102987010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-320","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:58.847195576Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-320","depends_on_id":"bd-2sr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:58.332596657Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-320","depends_on_id":"bd-3d0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:57.819318349Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-320","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:36:05.880390018Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-320","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:58:06.958842873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-320","depends_on_id":"bd-xgo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:45.142354946Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-331","title":"Dev UX: diagnostics + trace viewer for extensions","description":"# Goal
Improve **developer UX** with a focused diagnostics and trace viewer for extension runs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Standardized log schema for extension lifecycle + hostcalls.
- Pretty renderer for logs (human-readable summary + JSONL export).
- Error messages include: capability name, scope, and fix hints.

# Why (User Value)
- Faster iteration and fewer support questions for extension authors.
- Makes the Node/Bun-free approach feel *better*, not just smaller.

# Tests
- Snapshot tests for rendered output.
- E2E harness consumes and validates logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:47.585256915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:59.751720461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-331","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:59.751675497Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-331","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:59.268917216Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33v","title":"Unit tests: configuration loading + overrides","description":"# Unit tests: configuration loading + overrides

## Goal
Add comprehensive tests for configuration discovery, precedence, and error handling
using real files/env (no mocks).

## Scope
- Config file discovery order (project, user, global) and precedence.
- Env var overrides (PI_CONFIG_PATH, provider keys, session dirs).
- CLI flag overrides vs config file values.
- Invalid config handling (malformed JSON/TOML, unknown keys, bad types).
- Default values when config is missing.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log discovered config paths, chosen winner, and resolved values for key fields.
- On failure, dump parsed config + override stack (redacted).

## Determinism Requirements
- Use temp dirs with explicit file contents.
- No network access; avoid relying on real home dir.

## Files
- tests/config_loading.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ config tests
- [ ] Precedence order validated
- [ ] Env + CLI overrides validated
- [ ] Error messages are clear and actionable
- [ ] Logs include path resolution + resolved values","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:42.762280782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T08:34:47.598775641Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T08:34:47.598700090Z","close_reason":"Added config load tests + load_with_roots refactor","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33v","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:42.770190069Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33v","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:47.186437739Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-340x","title":"Message queue: keybindings + editor interactions","description":"# Goal
Implement the keybinding-driven UX for queueing and dequeuing messages.

# Required Behavior (legacy)
- Enter while busy: enqueue **steering** message.
- Alt+Enter while busy: enqueue **follow-up** message.
- Escape while busy: abort current work and restore queued messages into editor.
- Alt+Up: dequeue queued messages back into editor for editing.

# Dependencies
- Requires keybinding action layer (`bd-3ip`).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shortcuts work even when focus is not strictly the editor (as long as interactive is active).
- [ ] Dequeue restores messages in the correct order.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:38:15.766243305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:42:07.428330811Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-340x","depends_on_id":"bd-2skp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:38:15.774207225Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-340x","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:38:33.550525297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-346","title":"Workstream: Session Index Integration","description":"# Workstream: Session Index Integration

## Goal
Wire the existing SQLite session index (src/session_index.rs) into the CLI flow
for fast session discovery and resumption.

## Background
The session index module is fully implemented (360+ lines) but not used:
- SQLite-based index with WAL mode
- Fast lookups by path or CWD
- Session metadata extraction (message count, name, file size, mtime)
- Atomic operations with file locking

Currently, session operations scan the filesystem directly, which is slow
for users with many sessions.

## Current State

### What Exists (src/session_index.rs)
```rust
pub struct SessionIndex { db: SqliteConnection }

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> Result<Self>
    pub fn index_session(&self, session_path: &Path) -> Result<()>
    pub fn list_sessions(&self, cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn find_recent(&self, cwd: &str) -> Result<Option<SessionMeta>>
    pub fn reindex_all(&self) -> Result<()>
}

pub struct SessionMeta {
    pub path: PathBuf,
    pub cwd: String,
    pub name: Option<String>,
    pub message_count: u32,
    pub created_at: DateTime,
    pub modified_at: DateTime,
    pub file_size: u64,
}
```

### What's Missing
1. No calls to `index_session()` after session.persist()
2. No calls to `list_sessions()` in session picker
3. No calls to `find_recent()` for --continue flag
4. No index maintenance hooks

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Wire Index on Session Save
- After session.persist(), call session_index.index_session()
- Index path stored at ~/.pi/agent/sessions/index.db

### Phase 2: Use Index for Session Listing
- --resume flag queries index instead of filesystem walk
- Session picker uses indexed metadata
- Much faster for users with 100+ sessions

### Phase 3: Use Index for --continue
- --continue queries index for most recent by CWD
- Falls back to filesystem if index empty/stale

### Phase 4: Index Maintenance
- Background reindex on startup (if stale)
- Handle deleted sessions (prune index)
- Handle moved sessions (update paths)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Sessions indexed on save
- [ ] Session picker uses index
- [ ] --continue uses index
- [ ] Startup remains fast (<100ms)
- [ ] Handles index corruption gracefully

## Files Affected
- src/session_index.rs (already implemented)
- src/session.rs (add indexing calls)
- src/session_picker.rs (use index queries)
- src/main.rs (wire up index)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:19.943589682Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:37:00.112193624Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:37:00.112126038Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-346","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:19.950924399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-351","title":"Test coverage baseline + gap matrix (no-mock audit)","description":"Goal:
- Produce a single, self-contained coverage matrix for all core modules (src/*) showing existing tests, whether they are real (no mocks), and gaps for unit + integration + e2e.

Scope:
- Inventory tests under tests/ and #[cfg(test)] in src/.
- Mark any mocks/fakes/stubs and explain whether they are acceptable or must be replaced.
- Map each module to test types: unit, integration, conformance, e2e.
- Identify missing E2E flows and missing unit coverage hotspots (main.rs, config, compaction, session persistence, provider streaming, TUI, resources, package_manager).

Deliverables:
- docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md (or similar) with a table and brief narrative.
- A prioritized list of gaps that feeds into downstream beads.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Matrix is complete for all src/ modules and tests/ files.
- Explicitly flags all mock usage + recommended replacement path.
- Includes a minimal prioritized backlog with rationale.

Notes:
- Planning/audit only; no functional code changes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:05.803511426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:10:44.449582192Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:10:44.449520877Z","close_reason":"Completed coverage matrix doc","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-351","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:05.814604761Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-351","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started coverage audit. Created docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md with full src/tests inventory, mock audit (MockProvider in tests/rpc_mode.rs), and prioritized gaps (E2E CLI, provider VCR, extensions conformance, session_index/compaction/models, TUI snapshots, HTTP client integration).","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:10:06Z"}]}
